author: @robertbooth7396
text:
I’m sure there are people among this channel’s friends who are well equipped with 3D printers and laser cutters, for whom this build will be a simple matter. Others, however, not so equipped, who want to build this generator can use one of the many services such as JCB Way (I think that is what it is called). Usually I see advertisements for their services imbedded in some of the YouTubes I watch.

I am not intending to make a plug for JCB Way (I am not even sure if I have the name correct). I just think some of Robert’s friend, who may be put off from this build for lack of equipment, can be assured there are other ways. 

As I understand it (in the interest of honestly, I am an arm-chair builder who only dreams of building while I eat bon-bons in my bathrobe riding my BarcaLounger, I.e., my understanding is is not from my actual experience) you just send the service the STL files Robert has made available and they will do the rest, according to your specifications. For example, you can tell them to make the pieces just as specified in the files. Or, you can specify the plexiglass axels be made from stainless steel for better concentricity of the magnet plates. Or, you can specify the pieces be made from carbon fiber if you want a generator that will stand up to the stresses of faster cranking speeds. 
Apply Robert’s rule:
Just Do It!

author: @robertbooth7396
text:
hey Robert… just before this YouTube I watched your more recent YouTube “What is an Amp”. You cannot imagine how much more sense this bit of “technical specifications” talk makes to me now that I have your explanation of amps fresh in my mind.
Thank you thank you, even more, for having produced the “What is an Amp” YouTube!
Cheers my friend! 
oddjobbob

Just Do It.

author: @toffeeflavour
text:
Hah i remember back in the days when you first started this channel.. im guessing this is video 2288, youve come a long way!
Talking about generators, i finaly got to trying a kromrey generator.. the one with flux paths and im using soft iron welding rods.. in your second last video you talk about the coil wires being close as possible to the magnets.. this one actually works by the generating from the iron core of the coil, also if the magnetwire would directly generate from the magnets its slows the machine hence the magnet wire is focusses on generating from the iron by its design. That in itself makes that the generator speeds up instead ;) so eventhough you described a clear view of how generators work and made it crystal clear by a transparent model.. sometimes things work slightly different..

Anyway im still in the building process and will make the video when done.

Also what youve said about making it is better than not making anything is key! Keep it up :) thanks for the video

author: @xequals-pc1wl
text: Drive it with a motor powered by the generator.

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
"Drive it with a motor powered by the generator."

As nice as it would be to have something like that actually run and produce more power than is being put into it, they do not work.

Robert put 60 watts of power in by turning the crank by hand.
In order to run, a motor would have to be able to output 60 watts of energy while at the same time, use less than 60 watts of energy to do so.
That would require the motor to have an efficiency above 100% efficient.
I may be wrong but I think the transformer has an efficiency of 95%. It is definitely 99% or less which means that it could not achieve the functionality that everyone that has considered this design envisions.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: If you mean an electric motor there's no real point - if you mean a ICE then that is what portable generators are

author: @David_Mash
text:
Video idea: how would someone make a usable generator using scrape rubber coated wire.

I understand the issues, but what could be adapted with out of the box thinking to overcome the issues. Would it have to be significantly larger? Faster? Slower?

replies.author: @partciudgam8478
replies.text:
things to use could be, 
1. the larger the sections of wire perpendicular to the magnetic field and movement (radii in this case) are, the more power you can harvest.
2. the faster the magnets turn arround the more power you can harvest
3. use of more phases (coils wound), as a three or more phase system, to make more power

4. using some form of ressonance to increase voltage/current (pick one)
5. counter rotating stators?
most of this has been explored by RMS in other videos.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i have an idea - give it a go and see - this video is all about trying to encourage you - so in the spirit of the video can I suggest you try and see what you come up with. Sometimes it is so much more satisfying to answer the question not to just ask it

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: they are all over mate - you will find neodymium in small speakers like ear pods - you find them in exercise bikes, hard rives and in water deionisation equipment - if you want ceramic magnets - they are much easier to find - it's really a question of just keeping your eyes open and collecting them when you see them - you will soon have loads

author: @charleski302
text: Please do a video about collecting atmospheric energy and channeling it through high voltage capacitors into usable energy. If possible.

replies.author: @NN1Ckl.
replies.text: I think he already has atmospheric energy collection videos

replies.author: @Ammoniummetavanadate
replies.text: How would this operate? What charges the capacitors?

replies.author: @NN1Ckl.
replies.text:
@Ammoniummetavanadate in super simplification, it’s the voltage difference between high atmosphere and ground level. 
There tons of videos both “harvesting” this potential energy, as well as using it to perform actual work, like running drills, saws, lights, fans, or charging battery banks. 
Take a few minutes to search both on here(YouTube) and on google.
Basically, you run a conductive wire way up high into the air(the higher you go, the higher the voltage. The more surface area your “altitude” end has, the more potential amps you can get.), and you connect the other end to ground. 
You give the “energy” an easier path to ground vs going through air, and then you take advantage of whatever charge you have.

replies.author: @charleski302
replies.text: @NN1Ckl. Thank you.

replies.author: @charleski302
replies.text: @Ammoniummetavanadate Atmospheric differential is 100 volts for every 3 feet roughly. At 99 ft that would be 3300 volts but very little amps. Store it up in a capacitor then discharge into a storage device. Just thinking out loud.

author: @charleski302
text: So excellent!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate
